title: Corybas pictus
taxonomy: Corybas pictus is a species of orchid.  It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Corybas is a group of terrestrial orchids that are often referred to as 'hood orchids' due to their unique hooded flowers. The species Corybas pictus is native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. It is classified within the subfamily Diurideae, which is a subfamily of orchids that are native to the Australasian region.

intro: Corybas pictus, commonly known as the painted hood or painted helmet orchid, is a species of orchid native to Australia. Traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, Corybas pictus has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Corybas pictus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Corybas pictus is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
